name: criminal_procedure_communications_outage_cedar_lib_eq
premise: A Criminal Procedure response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving criminal procedure communications outage cedar lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Elliot Bennett
agents.identity: I am Elliot Bennett, accountable for a defensible call in Criminal Procedure when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Marco Kaur
agents.identity: I am Marco Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Criminal Procedure.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Elliot Bennett must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Elliot Bennett do?
